description: HP/Agilent 6625A System DC Power Supply, a multiple-output (662xA series) precision linear supply with HP-IB (IEEE-488) control. Each output is individually programmed and metered for voltage, current, voltage/current range, overvoltage protection, and overcurrent protection. The 6625A has 4 outputs; commands address an output channel as the first parameter (1-4).

commands.name: set_voltage
commands.description: Set the output voltage of a channel. Value is always in volts. The supply rounds the setting to the nearest multiple of its resolution.

commands.name: query_voltage_setting
commands.description: Query the programmed output voltage setting of a channel. Response is a real number (SZD.DDD).

commands.name: set_current
commands.description: Set the output current limit of a channel. Value is always in amps. The supply rounds the setting to the nearest multiple of its resolution.

commands.name: query_current_setting
commands.description: Query the programmed output current setting of a channel. Response is a real number.

commands.name: measure_voltage
commands.description: Query the measured (actual) output voltage of a channel. Response is a real number (SZD.DDD).

commands.name: measure_current
commands.description: Query the measured (actual) output current of a channel. Response is a real number.

commands.name: set_voltage_range
commands.description: Set the full-scale voltage programming range of a channel. The supply automatically selects the lowest range that contains the specified value.

commands.name: query_voltage_range
commands.description: Query the full-scale voltage programming range of a channel. Response is a real number (ZD.DDD).

commands.name: set_current_range
commands.description: Set the full-scale current programming range of a channel. The supply automatically selects the lowest range that contains the specified value.

commands.name: query_current_range
commands.description: Query the full-scale current programming range of a channel. Response is a real number.

commands.name: step_voltage
commands.description: Increase or decrease a channel's output voltage by a step value. A value out of range causes a number range error.

commands.name: step_current
commands.description: Increase or decrease a channel's output current by a step value. A value out of range causes a number range error.

commands.name: set_output_state
commands.description: Enable or disable an output channel. Does not disturb other programmed functions and does not reset protection circuits. 0 = OFF, 1 = ON.

commands.name: query_output_state
commands.description: Query whether an output channel is on or off. Response is an integer (ZZD).

commands.name: set_overvoltage
commands.description: Set the overvoltage (OV) trip value of a channel in volts.

parameters.name: voltage
parameters.range: See Table 5-4 (initial value 55 V; format SZZD.DD)

commands.name: query_overvoltage
commands.description: Query the overvoltage trip value of a channel. Response is a real number (SZZD.DD).

commands.name: reset_overvoltage
commands.description: Reset the overvoltage circuit and re-enable the output after an OV trip. Remove the overvoltage condition first, or the supply will fire the OV again. Does not clear the fault register.

commands.name: enable_overcurrent
commands.description: Enable or disable overcurrent protection on a channel. When enabled and the output enters +CC mode, OCP downprograms the output voltage and disables the output. 0 = OFF, 1 = ON.

commands.name: query_overcurrent
commands.description: Query whether overcurrent protection is enabled on a channel. Response is 0 (off) or 1 (on).

commands.name: reset_overcurrent
commands.description: Reset the output channel after an overcurrent trip and re-enable it. First disable OCP or reduce output current below the programmed limit. Does not clear the fault register.

commands.name: query_status
commands.description: Query the present status register of a channel. Response is an integer 0-255 decoded per Table 5-5 (CV, +CC, -CC, OV, OCP, UNR, coupled-parameter, etc.).

commands.name: query_accumulated_status
commands.description: Query the accumulated (astatus) status register of a channel, recording every status condition entered since last queried. Response is an integer 0-255 (Table 5-5). The register is reset to the present status value after being read.

commands.name: query_fault
commands.description: Query the fault register of a channel. A bit is set when the corresponding bit is set in both the status and mask registers. Response is an integer 0-255 (Table 5-5). The fault register is cleared after being read.

commands.name: set_mask
commands.description: Set (unmask) bits in a channel's mask register to specify which status conditions are enabled to set bits in the fault register. Value is a numeric code 0-255 per Table 5-5.

commands.name: query_mask
commands.description: Query the bits set in a channel's mask register. Response is an integer 0-255 (ZZD).

commands.name: query_error
commands.description: Query the present programming or hardware error. An error code number is returned over the GP-IB (see Table 5-8). The error register is cleared after being read.

commands.name: set_delay
commands.description: Set the reprogramming delay time of a channel, which delays the onset of CV/+CC/-CC/UNR fault detection after a setting change. Specified in seconds (0 to 32 s, in 4 ms steps). Power-on default is 20 ms.

commands.name: query_delay
commands.description: Query the reprogramming delay setting of a channel. Response format <sp>ZD.DDD seconds.

commands.name: set_display
commands.description: Turn the front panel display on or off. Turning the display off shortens command processing time. 0 = OFF, 1 = ON.

commands.name: display_string
commands.description: Display a quoted message string (max 12 characters) on the front panel.

commands.name: query_display
commands.description: Query whether the front panel display is on. Response is 1 (on) or 0 (off).

commands.name: set_meter
commands.description: Select which output channel the front panel display meters.

commands.name: query_meter
commands.description: Query which output channel the front panel display is currently metering.

commands.name: store_state
commands.description: Store the present operating state (VSET, VRSET, ISET, IRSET, OVSET, OCP, DLY) of all outputs into one of eleven non-volatile registers (0-10). At power-on the supply initializes all outputs from register 0.

commands.name: recall_state
commands.description: Recall the stored voltage, voltage range, current, current range and OV settings for all outputs from the specified register (0-10). Outputs are set sequentially (output 1, output 2, etc.).

commands.name: clear
commands.description: Return the power supply to its turn-on state. Clears the service request and the PON bit. May be sent as an addressed or unaddressed command.

commands.name: set_srq
commands.description: Set the causes for generating a service request (SRQ): 0 = none/off, 1 = on faults, 2 = on errors, 3 = on faults and errors.

commands.name: query_srq
commands.description: Query the present setting of the causes for issuing an SRQ. Response is 0, 1, 2, or 3.

commands.name: set_power_on_srq
commands.description: Enable or disable the power-on service request. 1 causes the supply to generate an SRQ at power-on or after a temporary loss of power; 0 disables it. Setting is retained in non-volatile memory.

commands.name: query_power_on_srq
commands.description: Query whether the power-on SRQ is enabled. Response is 1 (on) or 0 (off).

commands.name: identify
commands.description: Query the model number of the supply. Response is 'Agilent 662XA'.

commands.name: self_test
commands.description: Cause the power supply to perform a self-test of its GP-IB interface. Response is 0 if the test passes.

commands.name: query_rom
commands.description: Query the revision date of the supply's primary ROM.

commands.name: set_calibration_mode
commands.description: Turn calibration mode on or off. 0 = OFF, 1 = ON. (Calibration commands require CMODE on; see Appendix A.)

commands.name: query_calibration_mode
commands.description: Query whether calibration mode is on. Response is 1 (on) or 0 (off).
